title: 10 best herbal creams for dizziness

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Adiantum extract has antioxidant compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to dizziness and vertigo.
Circulatory effects: The plant contains flavonoids and saponins that may help improve blood circulation and reduce blood pressure, which can alleviate symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Adiantum extract has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear or other areas of the body, which can contribute to dizziness and balance disorders.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with dizziness because of its unique ability to improve blood circulation and oxygenation to the brain. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba extract have been shown to increase the supply of blood to the brain, which can help alleviate dizziness and lightheadedness. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may also help to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation, further contributing to its potential benefits for individuals experiencing dizziness. Regular use may lead to improved balance and reduced dizziness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (especially Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress that may contribute to dizziness.

Terpenoids (especially Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): These compounds have vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of dizziness.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, improving blood circulation and reducing the risk of dizziness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which is responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are another key component, used in creams for dizziness, as they contain ginkgotoxine, a compound that may help improve blood circulation and reduce dizziness.

Roots: Ginkgo biloba roots are also used, as they are rich in bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and terpenoids, which contribute to the herb's potential benefits for dizziness and other health issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the fall season when they turn yellow.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the leaves and obtain the liquid extract.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and melt them together.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ginkgo biloba liquid extract with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir until smooth.

remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Valerian root helps to dilate blood vessels, which can improve blood flow to the brain and reduce dizziness caused by inadequate circulation.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in valerian creams may help to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dizziness and vertigo.
Calming and sedative effects: Valerian root has a calming and sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate dizziness and balance issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with dizziness because of its sedative and calming properties. The root of the valerian plant contains valerenic acid, which interacts with the body's GABA receptors to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. This can help alleviate dizziness caused by stress, anxiety, or inner ear issues. By reducing the body's stress response, valerian creams can help stabilize balance and reduce the frequency of dizzy spells, providing relief and comfort to those affected.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid, a valeric acid derivative, contributes to its sedative and calming effects, which can help alleviate dizziness by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help stabilize blood pressure and reduce dizziness caused by inner ear problems or anxiety.

Valerenol: Valerenol, another sesquiterpene, has been demonstrated to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce dizziness by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and stabilizing blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high content of valerenic acids, which have a sedative and anti-anxiety effect.
Barks: Valerian barks are used because they contain valerenic acids and other compounds that can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are also used due to their presence of valerenic acids and other bioactive compounds that may help to alleviate dizziness and promote calmness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried valeriana officinalis root in late summer or early fall when the plant is fully ripe.
Combine the dried root with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 to 3 hours or until it reaches a temperature of 60 to 70°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the root particles.
Allow the herbal cream to cool and mature for 24 to 48 hours before applying it to the skin.

remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, the active compounds found in kava, have been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate dizziness and lightheadedness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal kava creams can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which are thought to contribute to dizziness and vertigo, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting cells from damage.
Muscle relaxants: Kava has been found to have a muscle-relaxing effect, which can be beneficial for individuals who experience dizziness due to muscle tension or spasms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Piper methysticum, also known as kava, creams helps with dizziness because of its ability to relax the nervous system and improve balance. The active compounds in kava, such as kavalactones, interact with neurotransmitters to reduce anxiety and stress, which are common causes of dizziness. By calming the mind and body, kava creams can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo, promoting a sense of stability and well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support overall balance and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, specifically kawain and demethylkawain, have been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate dizziness and nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Kaempferol: Kaempferol is a flavonoid that has been found to have anti-anxiety and anti-nausea properties, which can help reduce dizziness by calming the nervous system and stabilizing blood pressure.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Essential oils from Rhyzomes are used to create creams for dizziness due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Aqueous extracts from Leaves are used to make creams for dizziness because of their sedative effects.
Barks: Volatile oils from Barks are used in creams for dizziness due to their sedative and anti-anxiety properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried piper methysticum roots and place them in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried piper methysticum roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 4-6 hours at a low heat setting.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax is fully dissolved.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lavender's calming effects can help reduce anxiety-related dizziness, allowing individuals to feel more grounded and stable.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate symptoms associated with vestibular disorders, such as vertigo, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness.

Sedative and relaxing properties: The sedative effects of lavender can help promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are common triggers for dizziness, allowing individuals to feel more calm and centered.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with dizziness because of its calming properties and ability to promote relaxation. The fragrance of lavender oil has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by stress, anxiety, or other factors. By reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of calm, lavender creams can help stabilize the body and mind, making it easier to maintain balance and reduce feelings of dizziness. remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene with sedative and anti-anxiety properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce dizziness caused by stress or anxiety.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene with a sedative effect, which can help alleviate dizziness by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the body.
Lavandulol: A terpene with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by inflammation or irritation in the inner ear or other parts of the body.

Stems: While less commonly mentioned, the stems of Lavandula angustifolia may be used to extract additional lavender oil for its benefits in reducing dizziness and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers early in the morning when they are fully open.
Dry the fresh flowers in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 2 hours to preserve their fragrance.
Combine 10 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 15 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a storage container.

remedy_properties: Passionflower: Passionflower helps to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of dizziness and vertigo by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
Ginger: Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness by reducing inflammation in the inner ear and improving blood circulation.
Lavender: Lavender oil helps to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate dizziness caused by anxiety or panic attacks.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with dizziness because of its natural calming properties. The herb contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which work together to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. When applied topically, these compounds can help soothe the nervous system and calm the mind, alleviating symptoms of dizziness and vertigo. By promoting a sense of balance and calm, Passiflora incarnata creams can provide quick relief from dizziness and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Passiflora incarnata help with dizziness by exerting their anxiolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce anxiety and alleviate symptoms associated with dizziness.

Alkaloids (especially harmine and harmaline): The alkaloids, particularly harmine and harmaline, found in Passiflora incarnata contribute to its ability to alleviate dizziness by acting as central nervous system depressants, which can calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related symptoms.

Isosibirin and other terpenoids: The terpenoids, including isosibirin, present in Passiflora incarnata may help with dizziness by exhibiting sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can reduce stress and anxiety, thereby alleviating dizziness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system.
Fruits: The fruit pulp of Passiflora incarnata contains bioactive compounds that may help reduce symptoms of dizziness, nausea, and anxiety.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that may have a soothing effect on the nervous system and help alleviate dizziness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Steep 5 grams of dried flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture and combine it with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal oat cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like avenanthramides, which help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms of dizziness by calming irritated tissues and reducing swelling.

Soothing and calming properties: The moisturizing and emollient effects of herbal oat cream help calm and soothe the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort that can contribute to feelings of dizziness or lightheadedness.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal oat cream contains antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing the likelihood of experiencing dizziness and other symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oats, creams helps with dizziness because it contains a natural anti-inflammatory compound called avenanthramides. These compounds help to soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing inflammation and stress that can contribute to dizziness. Additionally, Avena sativa creams have been shown to improve blood circulation and balance, helping to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo. remedy_parts: Seeds: Avena sativa seeds are rich in beta-sitosterol, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate dizziness symptoms.
Leaves: Avena sativa leaves contain flavonoids and saponins that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe dizziness.
Roots: Avena sativa roots are a good source of saponins and flavonoids, which can help to calm the nervous system and alleviate dizziness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa roots and leaves in moderation and dry them for at least two weeks in a dark place.
Grind the dried avena sativa roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of avena sativa powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of preparation time.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the coconut oil mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated and solidifies within 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and dizziness caused by stress or other factors.
Sedative properties: The sedative effects of chamomile cream can help to soothe and relax the body, which can be especially helpful in alleviating symptoms of vertigo and dizziness.

The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate dizziness and vertigo. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is a common cause of dizziness. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) receptor agonist, which can help alleviate dizziness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.

Luteolin: As a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, luteolin may help alleviate dizziness by reducing inflammation in the inner ear and promoting blood flow to the affected area.

Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate dizziness by reducing inflammation and pain in the inner ear and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their calming and soothing properties, which help to alleviate dizziness and promote relaxation.
Leaves: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and calming effects, which can help to reduce dizziness caused by inflammation or stress.
Seeds: Utilized for their sedative and calming properties, which may help to alleviate dizziness and promote a sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers when they are in full bloom to obtain the highest concentration of active ingredients.
Dry the flowers in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at a temperature not exceeding 40 degrees celsius.
Infuse 25 grams of dried flowers in 250 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a preservative such as phenonip and a thickening agent like cetearyl alcohol in a ratio of 10 to 20 percent.

remedy_properties: Scutellaria flavonoids: These compounds have been shown to possess antispasmodic and sedative effects, which can help calm nervous system activity and alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Baicalin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear that may be contributing to dizziness and balance issues.
Scutellarein: This flavonoid has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress that may be exacerbating dizziness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis, also known as skullcap, creams helps with dizziness because of its calming and balancing properties. This helps to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo by reducing anxiety and stress levels, improving blood flow to the brain, and stabilizing the body's equilibrium. remedy_constituents: Baicalein: This flavonoid helps alleviate dizziness by acting as a vasodilator, which improves blood flow to the brain and helps regulate blood pressure.
Baicalin: As a flavonoid glycoside, baicalin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to dizziness and vertigo.
Wogonin: This flavone has sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which can help alleviate dizziness and vertigo by reducing nervous system activity and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high content of baicalein, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-anxiety properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used due to their presence of flavonoids, which have been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system and help alleviate dizziness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest scutellaria baicalensis roots in early autumn when the plants are fully mature and the roots are at their maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area at a temperature below 40 degrees celsius for 2-3 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle at a speed of 10,000 rpm.
Combine 250 grams of the scutellaria baicalensis root powder with 500 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat the mixture at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass container where it can be stored at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Vasoprotective compounds: Chaste tree cream contains vasoprotective compounds that help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce symptoms of dizziness caused by poor circulation or orthostatic hypotension.

Corticosteroid-inhibiting properties: The cream's corticosteroid-inhibiting properties may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate stress-related symptoms of dizziness, such as anxiety and nervousness.

Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds: Chaste tree cream contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation in the neck and head, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness or vertigo.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus castus, also known as chaste tree, creams helps with dizziness because it contains bioactive compounds that promote balance and equilibrium in the body. The herb is believed to have a regulating effect on the endocrine system, particularly on the hormonal balance of estrogen and progesterone. This helps to alleviate dizziness and lightheadedness caused by hormonal fluctuations, menopausal symptoms, and other conditions. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help alleviate dizziness by promoting balance and reducing anxiety, which can contribute to dizziness.

Vitexin: As a flavonoid, vitexin has antioxidant properties that can help mitigate oxidative stress, which may contribute to dizziness and other symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of iridoids, particularly aucubin and agnuside, which are believed to have a sedative effect on the nervous system and help alleviate dizziness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried vitex agnus castus flowers and leaves from a reputable supplier.
Combine the dried herbal material with 60 grams of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Whip 120 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of shea butter into the infused oil mixture.

remedy_properties: Ginger: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with dizziness, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms.
Peppermint: Peppermint's menthol content has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to ease the discomfort and dizziness caused by motion sickness or other gastrointestinal issues.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia x europaea, also known as lime, creams helps with dizziness because of its calming and sedative properties. The extract from this herb, also known as linden, has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial in reducing dizziness and lightheadedness. The anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds present in Tilia x europaea creams may also help to soothe and calm the nervous system, leading to a reduction in dizziness and a sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Terpenoids: These compounds help reduce dizziness by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety through their sedative effects on the nervous system.
Flavonoids (including Quercetin): Quercetin and other flavonoids in Tilia x europaea creams may help alleviate dizziness by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the blood vessels.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound may help alleviate dizziness by exerting a protective effect on the vestibular system, which is responsible for balance and equilibrium.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used in creams for dizziness due to their calming and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Buds: They are used in creams for dizziness due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
Flowers: They are used in creams for dizziness due to their calming and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried flowers in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for about 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove solids.
Mix the infused oil with distilled water in a 1:3 ratio by weight to create a solution.
Add a small amount of beeswax to the solution and heat it gently until the wax dissolves.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and set completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with dizziness because they contain calming compounds that soothe the nervous system. The hop extract in these creams has a sedative effect, which can help to slow down an overactive mind and reduce feelings of anxiety and unease. This, in turn, can alleviate dizziness and vertigo caused by stress or other factors. Additionally, hops creams can also improve blood flow and circulation, which can further contribute to reducing dizziness symptoms. By promoting relaxation and balance, herbal hop creams can provide effective relief from dizziness.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Hops contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating dizziness caused by hypertension or vasovagal reactions.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in hops cream may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to dizziness and vertigo symptoms.

Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: Hops have been traditionally used as a natural sedative and anti-anxiety agent, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to treat digestive issues and nausea, which can be related to dizziness.
Buds: Hops buds contain high levels of beta-caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating vertigo and dizziness.
Seeds: The seeds of the hops plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat anxiety, stress, and insomnia, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried humulus lupulus leaves and 4 ounces of carrier cream in a mortar and pestle.
Grind the humulus lupulus leaves into a fine powder using the mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground humulus lupulus powder with 4 ounces of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches 180°f after 15 minutes of heating.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a glass container, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort creams helps with dizziness because of its unique properties that calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of lightheadedness and vertigo. The herb's natural sedative compounds, such as leonurine and beta-sitosterol, work to regulate blood flow and circulation, alleviating dizziness caused by fluctuations in blood pressure or inner ear problems. Additionally, motherwort has anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe the brain and nervous tissues, further reducing symptoms of dizziness and promoting a sense of stability and balance.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Motherwort cream's vasodilatory properties help to dilate blood vessels, which can increase blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, alleviating symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in motherwort cream reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness by improving circulation and reducing blood vessel constriction.

Cardiac toning effects: Motherwort's cardiac toning properties help to strengthen the heart and improve its ability to pump blood efficiently, which can reduce symptoms of dizziness and fainting by regulating blood pressure and circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Motherwort roots are valued for their ability to calm the nervous system, making them a prime choice for treating dizziness-related issues.
Buds: The buds of the motherwort plant contain high levels of volatile oils that can help to soothe and stabilize the body's balance centers, alleviating feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leonurus sibiricus leaves and dry them for at least 30 minutes in shade.
Combine one teaspoon of dried leonurus sibiricus leaves with two tablespoons of olive oil in a small bowl.
Steam-distill the mixture using a double boiler for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Mix the extracted oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil, heating gently until the mixture reaches 180°f after five minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and let it cool before use, stored in an airtight container for six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with dizziness because they contain hyperforin, a potent phytochemical that has been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system. The cream absorbs into the skin and travels to the affected areas of the body, helping to calm and soothe the nerves. This can help alleviate feelings of dizziness, vertigo, and balance issues by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nerve function. Additionally, St John's wort has natural antispasmodic properties that can help ease muscle spasms in the neck and inner ear, which are common causes of dizziness.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have anticonvulsant and sedative effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of dizziness and vertigo by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce the frequency and severity of dizziness episodes by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has been found to have vasodilatory effects, which can help to increase blood flow to the brain and improve circulation, potentially reducing symptoms of dizziness caused by poor blood flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that helps to reduce anxiety and stress-related dizziness.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of vertigo.
Flowers: Used for their essential oils, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system and may help to stabilize blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum leaves by cutting stems at a 45-degree angle and collecting fresh material.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or using a dehydrator at 55°c.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 20% coconut oil and 80% beeswax, heating the mixture to 60°c for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Pour the mixture into sterile jars and let it cool at room temperature before sealing for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin creams helps with dizziness because it contains the natural antispasmodic properties of cumin, which help to relax and calm down the nervous system. The cream's soothing and calming effects can ease the symptoms of vertigo and dizziness by reducing muscle tension and promoting healthy circulation. Additionally, cumin has been known to stabilize blood pressure and reduce inflammation, both of which are common causes of dizziness. By applying the cream topically, individuals can quickly and naturally alleviate their symptoms of dizziness and regain a sense of balance and equilibrium.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cumin's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dizziness by reducing blood flow to the brain.

Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and stabilize blood vessels, alleviating symptoms of lightheadedness.

remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of cuminum cyminum seeds into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil in a glass bowl until well combined.
Add 2 drops of lavender essential oil and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir gently.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes or until warm and smooth.
Pour the cream into a sterilized glass jar and store it in refrigerator for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with dizziness because of its natural ability to stimulate blood flow and circulation. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to dilate blood vessels, allowing for increased oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the brain. This can help alleviate feelings of lightheadedness and dizziness by improving overall circulation and reducing symptoms of poor blood flow. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that may also contribute to its ability to relieve dizziness.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic Properties: Ginger's antiemetic compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea and dizziness by reducing the production of chemicals that stimulate vomiting.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the inner ear and body, which can contribute to dizziness and balance disorders, helping to promote a sense of stability and equilibrium.

Vasodilatory Properties: Ginger's vasodilatory compounds help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood circulation, which can increase oxygenation of the brain and nervous system, reducing symptoms of dizziness and vertigo.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem, is a good source of compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of dizziness.
Roots: Ginger roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which can help to calm the stomach and reduce feelings of dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2 inches of fresh ginger root and mix it with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Steam-distill the mixture for 20 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Combine the extracted liquid with 8 ounces of beeswax and 6 ounces of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax melts and blends smoothly.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and let it cool before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa damascena petals at dawn for their highest essential oil content.
Dry the petals in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours to preserve quality.
Combine 250g of dried petals with 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond or coconut and heat gently.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes under low heat then filter and discard solids.
Mix equal parts of the infused oil with beeswax and a few drops of lavender essential oil to create a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with dizziness because of its ability to promote blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. The natural oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have potent antioxidant properties that help to calm the nervous system and alleviate feelings of lightheadedness. Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of thyme help to relax muscle spasms and cramps that can cause dizziness, including those in the neck and shoulder muscles that can lead to vertigo.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves measure one ounce of dried herb or two ounces of fresh herb.
Combine the harvested thymus vulgaris with one-fourth cup of distilled water and let it steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with three tablespoons of beeswax pellets and two tablespoons of coconut oil heating gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers filling to the top and letting it cool and solidify completely taking approximately 30-45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with dizziness because its natural essential oils have a calming effect on the nervous system. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried origanum vulgare leaves and flowers for optimal potency and flavor.
Combine one part origanum vulgare herb with three parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the herbal mixture for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the oil turns a deep greenish-yellow color.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes until melted and blended.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the fresh melissa officinalis leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air-drying them for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt 200g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat while stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Mix the ground melissa officinalis powder with the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, stirring until well combined and smooth.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dizziness are topical applications containing plant-based ingredients that help alleviate dizziness and balance issues. These creams typically include herbs such as ginkgo biloba, ginger, and peppermint, which have natural anti-inflammatory and circulatory properties. For instance, Ginkgo Biloba Cream and Ginger Cream have shown to improve balance and reduce dizziness in individuals. By using these creams, people can regain stability and confidence in their daily activities, such as walking, driving, and even performing their jobs.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dizziness that are treated with herbal creams are nausea, vertigo, and lightheadedness. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as ginger, peppermint, and passionflower help alleviate these symptoms by calming the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Additionally, creams infused with essential oils like lavender and chamomile can also provide a sense of calm and relief from dizziness-like symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with dizziness is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and lavender. Peppermint helps to calm the digestive system and relieve nausea, while ginger reduces inflammation and stabilizes blood pressure. Lavender promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, which can contribute to dizziness. Applying these creams topically can provide relief from dizziness by reducing stress and promoting a sense of balance. This combination can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing vertigo or motion sickness.
